findings.evidence: Mathlib already has `theorem finite_setOf_absNorm_le [CharZero S] (n : ℕ) : {I : Ideal S | Ideal.absNorm I ≤ n}.Finite` in `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Norm/AbsNorm.lean:438`, and this PR proves the new theorem by `(Ideal.finite_setOf_absNorm_le N).subset fun _ hI => hI.2` at line 54.
findings.fix: Do not expose this as a new public API; keep the existing private helper or use `Ideal.finite_setOf_absNorm_le` directly at call sites.
findings.issue: `finite_ideal_absNorm_le_nat` publicly restates finiteness of ideals with bounded `Ideal.absNorm`, only adding the `I ≠ ⊥` subset condition.

summary: The count bounds appear to be new wrappers around TauCeti's existing real-valued bound, but one public finiteness theorem is just a special-case parallel API for an existing Mathlib theorem.
